title: Thai Smoked Lamb Kebabs
description: A flavorful twist on traditional kebabs, marrying the bold spices of Thai cuisine with the rich, smoky taste of lamb.

ingredients.name: Lamb shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: Make sure to cut the lamb into uniform pieces for even cooking.

ingredients.name: Smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: Smoked paprika adds a deep, smoky flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Thai red curry paste
ingredients.notes: This paste provides the bold, aromatic flavor of Thai cuisine.

ingredients.name: Fish sauce
ingredients.notes: Fish sauce adds a salty, umami flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Brown sugar
ingredients.notes: A small amount of brown sugar helps balance the sweetness and heat of the curry paste.

ingredients.name: Lime juice
ingredients.notes: Fresh lime juice adds a bright, citrusy flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Chopped cilantro
ingredients.notes: Fresh cilantro adds a fresh, herbaceous flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Thai basil
ingredients.notes: Thai basil adds a unique, slightly spicy flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: Coconut oil
ingredients.notes: Coconut oil helps add moisture and richness to the dish.

ingredients.name: Salt and pepper
ingredients.notes: Season the lamb with salt and pepper to bring out its natural flavors.

instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat.
In a small bowl, mix together the smoked paprika, Thai red curry paste, fish sauce, brown sugar, and lime juice.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the spice mixture.
Thread the lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the coconut oil onto the lamb and season with salt and pepper.
Grill the lamb kebabs for 8-10 minutes per side, or until they reach an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
Remove the kebabs from the grill and let them rest for 5 minutes.
Garnish with chopped cilantro, Thai basil, and a squeeze of lime juice.
Serve the lamb kebabs hot with your favorite sides, such as steamed rice or roasted vegetables.
